entities.name: MORINGA OLEIFERA
entities.description: Multipurpose tree with nutritional, therapeutic, and prophylactic properties of industrial importance

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: Variation in genetic material within a population, essential for crop improvement and selection of parental lines

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic effort to improve crop traits for better yield, quality, or resilience

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: The process of selecting promising parental lines to create superior hybrid offspring

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL MARKERS
entities.description: Physical characteristics used as tools for the evaluation of genetic diversity and crop improvement

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKERS
entities.description: DNA-based tools used for the evaluation of genetic diversity and conservation of plant genetic resources

entities.name: RAPD
entities.description: Random Amplified Polymorphic DNA markers used to determine genetic diversity among accessions

entities.name: SCOT MARKERS
entities.description: Start Codon Targeted markers used to determine genetic diversity among accessions

entities.name: ECONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Morphological characteristics of the plant that have industrial or agricultural value

entities.name: DENDROGRAM
entities.description: A tree-like diagram used to visualize the clustering of accessions based on phenotypic markers

entities.name: GEOGRAPHICAL HABITAT
entities.description: The physical area or region where plant accessions are naturally found or cultivated

entities.name: UPGMA APPROACH
entities.description: Unweighted Pair Group Method with Arithmetic Mean used to cluster genotypic data into a phylogenetic tree

entities.name: PHYLOGENETIC TREE
entities.description: A diagram representing the evolutionary relationships and clustering of accessions

entities.name: CROSS-POLLINATION
entities.description: The transfer of pollen between different plants, facilitating gene flow in populations

entities.name: POPULATION STRUCTURE ANALYSIS
entities.description: An analysis used to identify subpopulations within a group of accessions

entities.name: EXPECTED HETEROZYGOSITY
entities.description: A measure of genetic variation within subpopulations

entities.name: R-BASED STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: Computational analysis using R software to determine population structure and heterozygosity

entities.name: AMOVA
entities.description: Analysis of Molecular Variance used to attribute genetic variations to within- and between-populations

entities.name: PCA
entities.description: Principal Component Analysis used to visualize the distribution of genotypes in a scatterplot

entities.name: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Genetic material of plant origin of actual or potential value for conservation and breeding

relationships.description: The study assessed the genetic diversity among accessions of this crop
Genetic diversity is a cornerstone of any crop improvement program
Crop improvement programs use genetic diversity for the selection of parental lines in hybrid breeding
Morphological markers are used as tools for the evaluation of genetic diversity
Molecular markers are used as tools for the evaluation of genetic diversity
RAPD markers were used to determine genetic diversity in Moringa oleifera accessions
SCoT markers were used to determine genetic diversity in Moringa oleifera accessions
Significant morphological variations were noted for economic traits in Moringa oleifera accessions
The dendrogram indicated clustering of accessions from a shared geographical habitat
The UPGMA approach was used to separate accessions into clusters within the phylogenetic tree
Rapid gene flow through cross-pollination influences the clustering patterns of genetic diversity
Population structure analysis was used to identify three subpopulations of Moringa oleifera accessions
R-based structural analysis was used to determine the expected heterozygosity of subpopulations
AMOVA was used to attribute genetic variations to within- and between-populations
PCA suggested huge genetic variation among the Moringa oleifera population
Genetic diversity can be harnessed to conserve plant genetic resources
